root_text.sn56.46:1.1: “Atthi, bhikkhave, lokantarikā aghā asaṁvutā andhakārā andhakāratimisā, yatthamimesaṁ candimasūriyānaṁ evaṁmahiddhikānaṁ evaṁ mahānubhāvānaṁ ābhāya nānubhontī”ti.
root_text.sn56.46:2.1: Evaṁ vutte, aññataro bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn56.46:2.2: “mahā vata so, bhante, andhakāro, sumahā vata so, bhante, andhakāro.
root_text.sn56.46:2.3: Atthi nu kho, bhante, etamhā andhakārā añño andhakāro mahantataro ca bhayānakataro cā”ti?
root_text.sn56.46:2.4: “Atthi kho, bhikkhu, etamhā andhakārā añño andhakāro mahantataro ca bhayānakataro cā”ti.
root_text.sn56.46:3.1: “Katamo pana, bhante, etamhā andhakārā añño andhakāro mahantataro ca bhayānakataro cā”ti?
root_text.sn56.46:3.2: “Ye hi keci, bhikkhu, samaṇā vā brāhmaṇā vā ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānanti …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānanti,
root_text.sn56.46:3.5: abhisaṅkharitvā jātandhakārampi papatanti, jarandhakārampi papatanti, maraṇandhakārampi papatanti, sokaparidevadukkhadomanassupāyāsandhakārampi papatanti.
root_text.sn56.46:3.6: Te na parimuccanti jātiyā jarāya maraṇena sokehi paridevehi dukkhehi domanassehi upāyāsehi.
root_text.sn56.46:4.1: Ye ca kho keci, bhikkhu, samaṇā vā brāhmaṇā vā ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānanti …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānanti,
root_text.sn56.46:4.4: anabhisaṅkharitvā jātandhakārampi nappapatanti, jarandhakārampi nappapatanti, maraṇandhakārampi nappapatanti, sokaparidevadukkhadomanassupāyāsandhakārampi nappapatanti.
root_text.sn56.46:4.5: Te parimuccanti jātiyā jarāya maraṇena sokehi paridevehi dukkhehi domanassehi upāyāsehi.
root_text.sn56.46:5.1: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yogo karaṇīyo …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yogo karaṇīyo”ti.

translation_text.sn56.46:1.1: “Mendicants, the boundless gloom of interstellar space is so utterly dark that even the luminosity of the moon and the sun, so mighty and powerful, makes no impression.”
translation_text.sn56.46:2.1: When he said this, one of the mendicants asked the Buddha,
translation_text.sn56.46:2.2: “Sir, that darkness really is mighty, so very mighty.
translation_text.sn56.46:2.3: Is there any other darkness more mighty and terrifying than this one?”
translation_text.sn56.46:3.2: “There are ascetics and brahmins who don’t truly understand about suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the path.
translation_text.sn56.46:3.3: They take pleasure in choices that lead to rebirth …
translation_text.sn56.46:3.4: They continue to make such choices …
translation_text.sn56.46:3.5: Having made such choices, they fall into the darkness of rebirth, old age, and death, of sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress.
translation_text.sn56.46:3.6: They’re not freed from rebirth, old age, and death, from sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress.
translation_text.sn56.46:3.7: They’re not freed from suffering, I say.
translation_text.sn56.46:4.1: There are ascetics and brahmins who truly understand about suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the path.
translation_text.sn56.46:4.2: They don’t take pleasure in choices that lead to rebirth …
translation_text.sn56.46:4.4: Having stopped making such choices, they don’t fall into the darkness of rebirth, old age, and death, of sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress.
translation_text.sn56.46:4.5: They’re freed from rebirth, old age, and death, from sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress.
translation_text.sn56.46:4.6: They’re freed from suffering, I say.
translation_text.sn56.46:5.1: That’s why you should practice meditation …”
